findings.summary: ECP5 FPGA board with SDRAM, SPI flash, HDMI output, 3x USB-C, SD card; HDMI interface missed entirely, ECP5 power domains incomplete, cross-domain warnings are false positives

correct.description: Three TLV62569DBV switching regulators correctly identified with feedback dividers: U2 (+5V->+1V1), U3 (+5V->+2V5), U4 (+5V->+3V3)
Voltage divider ratios and estimated Vout values accurate: U2=1.1V (R6/R7), U3=2.515V (R8/R9), U4=3.327V (R10/R11) with Vref=0.6V
W9825G6KH SDRAM (U11) and W25Q128JVS SPI flash (U12) memory interfaces correctly detected with proper processor connections to ECP5 (U1)
Four TPD2EUSB30A ESD protection ICs (U5, U7, U8, U10) correctly identified with protected differential pairs
50MHz active oscillator Y1 (SG-8018CG) correctly identified with GLOBAL_CLK output net
I2C bus correctly detected with dual-domain pull-ups: 4.7k to +5V (R16/R17) and 3.3k to +3V3 (R18/R19) through PCA9306DC1 level translator (U6)
SPI flash bus detected with MOSI/MISO signals between U1 and U12
15 differential pairs correctly detected including USB and GPDI TMDS pairs
3 USB Type-C connectors (J3, J4, J5) correctly identified with CC pull-down and ESD compliance checks
PCA9306DC1 (U6) correctly identified as level translator for I2C between 3.3V and 5V domains
All 8 hierarchical sheets correctly traversed: root, io, sdram, power, usb, sd, flash, gpdi
Total component count of 162 matches schematic (50 resistors, 54 capacitors, 12 ICs, 6 LEDs, 6 connectors, etc.)
Decoupling analysis correctly covers all 4 power rails with accurate cap counts and values
UART bus detected between FT231XQ (U9) and ECP5 (U1) on USB_TXD/USB_RXD nets
Power sequencing correctly identifies all 3 regulators as always-on (EN tied to +5V)

incorrect.description: Cross-domain level shifter warnings for JTAG/UART signals (JTAG_TDI, JTAG_TDO, JTAG_TCK, JTAG_TMS, USB_TXD, USB_RXD, ~{USB_RTS}, ~{USB_DTR}) between U1 (+3V3) and U9 (+5V) are false positives. The FT231XQ's VCCIO pin (pin 20) is powered from its own 3V3OUT (pin 10), not +5V. Both ICs operate at 3.3V I/O, so no level shifter is needed.

U1 ECP5 FPGA power_rails shows only ['+3V3'] but it actually uses 3 rails: +1V1 (VCC core, 12 caps), +3V3 (VCCIO, 24 caps), and FILTERED_2V5 (VCCAUX, via FB1). The +1V1 and FILTERED_2V5 connections are missed in ic_power_rails.

FILTERED_2V5 net classified as 'signal' in net_classification, but it is a filtered power rail (2.5V through ferrite bead FB1) supplying ECP5 VCCAUX pins. Should be classified as 'power' or 'power_filtered'.

R42/C44 detected as an RC low-pass filter (1.59kHz) but this is actually the FT231XQ's 3V3OUT output decoupling. R42 (1k) connects D14 (LED anode) to 3V3OUT, while C44 (100nF) is decoupling on the 3V3OUT/VCCIO/~{RESET} net. Not a signal filter.

IC pin analysis shows connected_pins=0, no_connect_pins=0, unconnected_pins=0 for every IC. This appears to be a bug since all ICs clearly have connected pins (e.g., U1 has 80+ signal pins, U9 has 21 pins all connected).

missed.description: HDMI/DVI (GPDI) interface not detected. J2 is a GPDI Type-C connector (HDMI mini variant) with 4 TMDS differential pairs (D0+/-, D1+/-, D2+/-, CLK+/-) AC-coupled through 22nF capacitors (C36-C43) from ECP5 FPGA. The lib symbol has ki_keywords='hdmi conn' and ki_fp_filters='HDMI*C*'. This is a complete HDMI output interface.

JTAG debug interface not detected. FT231XQ (U9) uses modem control signals for bitbang JTAG: ~{RI}->TDI, ~{CTS}->TDO, ~{DSR}->TCK, ~{DCD}->TMS, connected to ECP5 (U1) dedicated JTAG pins. This is a standard FTDI-based JTAG programming interface with test points (TP9-TP12).
SPI flash bus missing CLK signal (FLASH_CLK net between U12.CLK and U1.CCLK) and CS signal (~{FLASH_CS} net between U12.~{CS} and U1.CSSPIN). Only MOSI/MISO detected.
SD card interface not detected as a bus. J6 (Micro_SD_Card_Det2) connects to ECP5 (U1) with full 4-bit SD bus: SD_CLK, SD_CMD, SD_DAT0-3, plus card detect (SD_DET).
Ferrite bead FB1 filtering the +2V5 rail to create FILTERED_2V5 for ECP5 VCCAUX not identified in any signal path detector. This is a common FPGA power supply filtering pattern.
Raspberry Pi compatible GPIO header (J1, Conn_02x20, 40-pin) not identified. This is a Raspberry Pi Zero form-factor board with 28 GPIO signals routed to the ECP5 FPGA.

suggestions: Add HDMI/DVI interface detection that matches GPDI connectors by ki_keywords containing 'hdmi' and/or TMDS-style differential pairs (D0+/D0-, D1+/D1-, D2+/D2-, CLK+/CLK-) with AC coupling capacitors
Detect JTAG interfaces by matching TDI/TDO/TCK/TMS net name patterns connected to FPGA/MCU dedicated JTAG pins, even when driven through FTDI bitbang rather than a dedicated JTAG controller
Track IC power rails across all multi-unit symbols; the ECP5 spans 10 units and has pins on +1V1, +3V3, and FILTERED_2V5 but only +3V3 is reported
Classify nets connecting to VCCAUX/VCC power_in pins through ferrite beads as filtered power rails, not signals
When determining U9 FT231XQ power domain, check VCCIO pin connection (which determines I/O voltage) rather than only VCC; VCCIO connects to 3V3OUT making it a 3.3V I/O device
Add SPI CLK and CS signal detection to complement MOSI/MISO in bus analysis
Detect SD/SDIO interfaces by matching SD_CLK/SD_CMD/SD_DAT* net name patterns connected to an SD card connector

findings.summary: Icepi Zero v1.3 is a Lattice ECP5 FPGA SBC with RPi-compatible GPIO, 256Mbit SDRAM, 128Mbit SPI flash, GPDI video output, and three USB-C ports. Analyzer correctly identifies most subsystems but has a false-positive level-shifter flag for FT231X signals, incomplete FPGA power domain tracking, and misses the GPDI/HDMI-compatible video interface.

correct.description: U1 (ECP5-CABGA256) correctly identified as FPGA with custom lib icepi-lib:LFE5U-25F-6BG256x
U12 (W25Q128JVS) SPI flash correctly detected with 6 shared signal nets to U1
U11 (W9825G6KH) SDRAM correctly detected with 39 shared signal nets to U1
Three TLV62569DBV switching regulators U2/U3/U4 correctly identified with inductors and feedback dividers from +5V
Y1 (SG-8018CG 50MHz MEMS) correctly detected as active_oscillator on GLOBAL_CLK
SPI flash bus correctly detected: FLASH_MOSI/FLASH_MISO between U12 and U1
I2C bus for GPDI DDC correctly detected with U6 (PCA9306DC1) level translator
ESD protection U5/U7/U8/U10 (TPD2EUSB30A) correctly identified on USB differential pairs
GPDI differential pairs correctly detected from U1
UART bridge U9 (FT231XQ) correctly detected with USB_TXD/USB_RXD to U1
USB-C compliance checks correctly passing CC1/CC2 pull-downs and ESD; VBUS ESD fail is accurate

incorrect.description: cross_domain_signals flags JTAG and UART signals as 'needs_level_shifter: true' between U1 (+3V3) and U9 (+5V). False positive: U9 (FT231XQ) has VCCIO tied to its own 3V3OUT pin, so all I/O switches at 3.3V.
power_domains for U1 (ECP5) lists only ['+3V3'], omitting +1V1 (VCC core) and FILTERED_2V5 (VCCAUX). ECP5 requires three supply rails.
power_budget for +2V5 shows ic_count=0 and load=0mA. U1 VCCAUX is supplied from +2V5 through FB1 (producing FILTERED_2V5). Load missed because filtered net doesn't carry the power symbol.

missed.description: GPDI/HDMI-compatible interface not detected: J2 carries complete DVI-compatible differential video (GPDI_D0-2+/-, GPDI_CLK+/-) plus DDC I2C, HPD, CEC, UTIL from U1.

suggestions: Fix FT231X level-shifter false positive: when VCCIO is connected to device's own regulated output (3V3OUT), use VCCIO voltage for signal domain classification.
Fix power_domains to aggregate across all units of multi-unit components (ECP5 has 10 units).
Fix power_budget to follow one-hop filtered rails through ferrite beads.
Add GPDI/TMDS interface detection: 4 differential pairs with D0/D1/D2/CLK naming plus HPD/DDC.
